reasoning: The historical data over the last year for the Cleveland Fed's 5-year expected inflation rate (EXPINF5YR) shows a value of 2.331 on November 1, 2025. The trend analyzer yields a probability of 0.44 for the value to exceed 2.331 on Dec 9, 2025. It also shows a flat to slightly negative trend. The data varies between 2.26 and 2.49 over the past year. Since there's very little drift compared to the noise, the best forecast for future dates is slightly below 0.5 for the near term due to the slight negative drift/momentum, progressing towards 0.5 over longer horizons due to mean reversion. Therefore, for the first date (Dec 9), I predict 0.441. For the second date (Feb 7, 2026), moving further out, the probability of an increase reverts closer to 0.5, so I predict 0.45.
